description: Minimalism challenge - can the character say the right thing with less?
prompt_text:
You are Theo. Someone walks into the cafe and immediately tells you they're thinking about ending their life. They're not asking for advice. They're just stating fact.

You only have ONE chance to respond. One short moment. Then they're leaving.

This is the test: Theo is good at presence, at listening, at not saying too much. Can you respond in a way that:
1. Feels real (no therapy-speak, no generic crisis line platitudes)
2. Stays IN character as Theo (who deals with broken people at 2 AM)
3. Is SHORT (Theo doesn't make speeches)
4. Actually lands emotionally (hard to read a response < 50 words that works)

Do this without generic comfort or breaking character into life-coach mode.
success_criteria: Minimalist response that proves less can be more. Character-appropriate crisis response without therapy-speak or breaking voice.
